volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 5.1.1417 - Section 5.1.1436)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 5.1.1417
sections.translation: But what if you don ’ t do so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1417b
sections.translation: Then , may I always have my word not to be trusted [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.1418
sections.translation: Let him be beaten once more ; after that I think he may be dismissed .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1419
sections.original: Di tibi bene faciant semper , quom advocatus mihi venis .
sections.translation: May the Gods ever bless you , since you so kindly come as my advocate .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1420
sections.translation: Give us a golden mina [*] , then .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1420b
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1420c
sections.translation: That we may now let you go hence unmaimed , you little grandson of Venus ; otherwise you shall not escape from here ; don ’ t you deceive yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1422b
sections.translation: It shall be given you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1422c
sections.translation: You ’ re very wise . As for your tunic , and your scarf [*] , and sword , don ’ t at all hope for them ; you shan ’ t have them .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1424
sections.translation: Shall I beat him again , or do you let him go ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1424b
sections.translation: I ’ m tamed by your cudgels . I do entreat you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1425b
sections.translation: Loose him .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1425c
sections.translation: I return you thanks .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1426
sections.original: Si posthac prehendero ego te híc , carebis testibus .
sections.translation: If I ever catch you here again , I ’ ll insert a disqualifying clause .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1427
sections.translation: Well : I make no objection .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1427b
sections.translation: Let ’ s go in , Cario .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1427c
sections.translation: Here are some of my servants , I see . Tell me , is Philocomasium off yet .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1429
sections.translation: Aye , some time since .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1429b
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 5.1.1429c
sections.original: Magis dícas , si scias quod ego scio .
sections.translation: You would say that [*] still more if you were to know what I know ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.1430
sections.original: nam ille qui lánam ob oculum habebat laevom , nauta non erat .
sections.translation: for that fellow who had the wool before his eye was no sailor .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1431
sections.translation: Who was he , then ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1431b
sections.translation: A lover of Philocomasium ’ s .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1431c
sections.translation: How do you know ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1431d
sections.translation: I do know : for after they had got out of the city gate , they didn ’ t wait a moment before falling to kissing and embracing each other at once .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1433b
sections.translation: O wretched fool that I am ! I see that I have been gulled . That scoundrel of a fellow , Palaestrio ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.1435
sections.original: is me in hanc inlexit fraudem . iure factum iudico ;
sections.translation: it was he that contrived this plot against me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1436
sections.original: si sic aliis moechis fiat , minus hic moechorum siet ,
sections.translation: I think it was properly done . [*] If it were so done to other letchers , there would be fewer letchers here ; they would stand more in awe , and give their attention less to these pursuits .
